title: The Town Mouse and the Country Mouse
content:
A Town Mouse once visited a cousin who lived in the country. The Country Mouse was simple and loved his friend dearly.

Beans and bacon, cheese and bread were all he had to offer, but he offered them freely. The Town Mouse looked at the simple food and turned up his long nose.

"I cannot understand, Cousin, how you can live on such food," he said. "Come with me to the town, and I will show you how to live."

When they reached the town, the Town Mouse took his friend into a grand dining room. There they found the remains of a fine feast.

There were jellies and cakes, and everything a mouse could wish for.

But just as the Country Mouse was about to nibble a dainty bit of pastry, in came the cat!

The two mice ran as fast as they could to a tiny hole in the wall.

"Let us try again," said the Town Mouse when the cat had gone.

They crept back to the dining room. But soon they heard a loud barking at the door. It was the dogs of the house!

Once again, they ran for their lives.

"Good-bye, Cousin," said the Country Mouse. "You may have good things to eat, but you eat them in fear."

"I would rather eat beans and bread in peace than cakes and pies in fear."

Better a simple life in peace than a rich life in fear.

The End.

author: Aesop
